summary: Completed backend security testing for OWASP Top 10 remediation (iteration 22). All 17 tests passed (100%). Verified: 1) CSV injection prevention in bulk user import, events bulk upload, and dining menu bulk upload - formula characters (=, +, -, @) are properly sanitized, 2) Access control - unauthorized users (unauthenticated and students) cannot access /api/admin/users and /api/tenants endpoints, 3) Super admin can access tenant management endpoints, 4) Login returns proper 401 for invalid credentials, 5) Role-based access control - students cannot access admin endpoints.

critical_code_review_comments: ✓ sanitize_csv_value function correctly strips formula trigger characters (=, +, -, @, \t, \r, \n)
✓ Function implemented consistently across admin.py, events.py, and dining.py
✓ Access control properly enforces role-based permissions
✓ Login endpoint returns proper 401 for invalid credentials (prevents credential enumeration)
✓ Super admin can access tenant management endpoints
✓ Tenant admin can access admin endpoints for their tenant
seed_data_creation: Created test users, events, and menu items with CSV injection payloads for verification
context_for_next_testing_agent: Security audit remediation testing complete. Key findings: 1) CSV injection prevention working correctly - sanitize_csv_value function strips formula characters (=, +, -, @, tab, newline) from all CSV bulk upload endpoints, 2) Access control properly enforced - unauthenticated returns 401, unauthorized role returns 403, 3) Login returns 401 for invalid credentials, 4) All three test credentials verified working. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_security_owasp.py
